hero.name: AJ Curry
hero.intro: Emmy Award-winning marketing executive with 12 years of experience building social, content, experiential, and influencer strategies for some of the most iconic brands in sports and entertainment. Known for creating revenue-driving social programs, scaling influencer networks to **3B+ reach**, turning cultural moments into measurable growth, and breaking the internet.
hero.imageAlt: AJ Curry pointing at the camera

items.text: Winner of the **2019 Emmy Award** for Outstanding Trans-Media Sports Coverage
Was a lead on the team that launched the **officially licensed NFT product**, NFL ALL DAY
Named to The Athletic’s NFL **40 Under 40** List
**Produced a national commercial** for the NFL which aired across primetime games
Helped lead the first ever music partnership to **bring Quavo to NBA Top Shot**
Conceptualized and produced the **NFL’s Showtime Cam**, sponsored by Twitter and Bud Light, which won the Clio grand award for best in Social Media
Created the **NFL’s first-ever Emerging Platforms team** to manage’s the NFL’s social presence on new, Gen-Z focused channels
Featured speaker and panelist at **SXSW, NFL Network’s Total Access, Twitter’s Sports Summit**, and more.
Served as a lead producer for the NFL’s first-ever Draft-A-Thon livestream, contributing to the **100M raised by the NFL for COVID relief**
Helped **print tweets on Super Bowl confetti** in partnership with Twitter
Led the **community strategy across products** for Dapper Labs
**Taught the Commissioner** the Toosie Slide which he debuted on the NFL’s TikTok account

sections.imageAlt: AJ Curry at the NFL Draft
sections.headline: Turning cultural moments into measurable growth.

sections.imageAlt: AJ Curry on a red carpet media line
sections.quote: From organizing epic IRL events, to negotiating six figure partnerships, to working with athletes and finding authentic collaborations, AJ’s blend of elite creativity and pragmatism makes her an ideal teammate and true game changing talent.
sections.citation: Jacob Eisenberg, Senior Leader at Dapper Labs

roles.title: Vice President, Social Content
roles.desc: Elevated to Vice President with a key focus on Thrill Sports and its properties, Power Slap, Nitro Circus, and Street League Skateboarding, overseeing the marketing, social, and content teams.

roles.title: Sr Director, Social Content Strategy
roles.desc: Served as the lead social, marketing, influencer, and content operating partner for the entire Fiume Capital portfolio.

roles.title: Marketing / Community Lead
roles.desc: Oversaw development, marketing, athlete engagement, and community growth of the NFL’s flagship product with Dapper.

roles.title: Senior Manager, Social Content
roles.desc: Led cross-platform social innovation and tentpole content activations for the NFL, launching new channels, campaigns, and creator programs to drive youth engagement and platform partnerships.

roles.title: Manager, Social Content
roles.desc: Oversaw creative production and original content strategy for NFL social channels, leading a multi-faceted creative team and driving high-impact storytelling.

roles.title: Social Content Producer
roles.desc: Launched the NFL’s official Snapchat Discover channel, and created original graphics, animations, and live content for NFL social with a key focus on driving engagement through innovative storytelling.

roles.title: Manager, Sports Marketing
roles.desc: Managed social strategy and content for 20+ top-tier professional athletes, driving growth through consistent brand development, always-on programming, and authentic fan engagement.

sections.imageAlt: AJ Curry shooting a player in the end zone
sections.quote: She is brilliant, hard-working, creative and makes everyone around her better. Not only does AJ dream big, but she also executes big visions and brings them to life at the highest quality possible.
sections.citation: Hali Stark, Head of Partnership Marketing at Dapper Labs

items.title: Dana White, Ronda Rousey Achieve 100M Milestone Hours Before UFC 317
items.desc: If Dana White wanted to draw attention to UFC 317, he chose the ideal accomplice—and no, not the fighters at Power Slap.

items.title: ‘More views than Taylor Swift’ — Footage of Power Slap star blowing kiss after eating hit breaks the internet
items.desc: Power Slap continues to rack up a ridiculous amount of social media views.

items.title: How the NFL Competes for—and Wins Over—Younger Fans’ Attention
items.desc: The league’s strategy includes transforming its players into influencers.

items.title: TikTok Dives Deeper Into Long-Form Sports Content
items.desc: Through live streams and content series, the social media platform has recently explored these efforts with sports brands like Jordan Brand and the NFL.

items.title: The NFL Doubles Down on its Snapchat Strategy as Other Media Partners Scale Back
items.desc: The NFL says its audience doubled viewership of its highlights video to 2 million this year.

items.title: Sports Leagues Helping Fans Escape Through TikTok
items.desc: With few live sports events going on, the NBA, NFL, NHL, and MLB are each using TikTok to distract fans from their uncertain realities.

items.title: Sports Brands Learning To Be Musically Creative On TikTok
items.desc: TikTok’s recent music regulations are forcing digital and social media workers in sports to be more creative with their content.

items.title: How the NFL used TikTok to Build Authentic Fan Connections
items.desc: The pro football league is generating fan engagement on TikTok comments.

items.title: NFL Tackles Social Justice On “Real Talk” Snapchat Show
items.desc: NFL, Snapchat come together with launch of social justice show, “Real Talk.”

items.title: KFC, American Eagle, and other household names break down how they built their TikTok followings
items.desc: The 42-minute recorded session broke down in detail why TikTok is the place to advertise — and included plenty of case studies on how to make it big on TikTok.

items.title: How Ocean Spray, NFL embed nostalgia in TikToks to tap ‘culture-defining’ moments
items.desc: Panelists at SXSW detailed how to initiate a strong strategy, from mastering the platform’s nuances to understanding what drives its community to engage.

items.title: How Iconic Brands Use TikTok To Engage With Fans: SXSW 2021 Edition
items.desc: During this year’s SXSW Conference, there was one session that really stood out to me, and that was “Driving Culture Through Content.”

items.title: How A TikTok Video Changed Arby’s Marketing, and Other Brand Stories from SXSW
items.desc: TikTok brings marketers from Arby’s, NFL, Ocean Spray, HBO Max, McDonald’s and Gatorade to virtual festival.

items.title: Bradley Grad Curry Part Of History Making Super Bowl For Women
items.desc: Flash forward seven years, AJ is the senior manager of social content for the NFL with five Super Bowl’s under her belt.

items.title: Clubhouse and the NFL Team Up for the Draft
items.desc: It’s the audio platform’s first partnership with a sports league.

items.title: For the First Time, a Woman Called the Shots at the Super Bowl
items.desc: NFL referee Sarah Thomas became the first woman to ever officiate a Super Bowl game.

items.title: Driving Culture Through Content | SXSW 2021
items.desc: Learn how brands are navigating TikTok’s participatory nature to seize their own cultural moments and connect with an engaged community in an authentic way.

items.title: Ballerz Nation #3: AJ Curry of NFL All Day
items.desc: NFL All Day Community Lead, AJ Curry, steps up to quarterback the Guest spot in episode #3 of Ballerz Nation.

items.title: “It starts with us. It starts with our allies. It starts with our leadership.”
items.desc: Colleen Wolfe, Aisha Chaney, AJ Curry, and Stacey Dales on creating environments in sports where women are supported and heard.

items.title: “I want there to be more of me. I’m tired of being the only one.”
items.desc: Colleen Wolfe, Aisha Chaney, AJ Curry, and Stacey Dales discuss the many obstacles of being a woman in a male-dominated sports industry.

items.title: “If you see something wrong, speak up. Say something.”
items.desc: Colleen Wolfe, Aisha Chaney, AJ Curry, and Stacey Dales discuss the importance of allies and how we can better support women in the workplace.
